title: Will President Trump send ICE agents to Boston’s Logan Airport?
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: President Trump may send ICE agents to Logan Airport due to a partial government shutdown affecting TSA staffing.
Over 400 TSA workers resigned due to the shutdown, affecting security at airports nationwide, except at Logan so far.
AFGE union leaders are inquiring about potential ICE deployment, emphasizing maintaining public safety at Logan Airport.

title: Is Bruins goalie Jeremy Swayman a legitimate Vezina candidate? The numbers speak for themselves.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman's performance boosts Bruins with 42 saves in a 4-2 win over the Red Wings.
Swayman ranks second in NHL for goals saved above expected with 27.1, sparking Vezina Trophy consideration.
Bruins struggle defensively, ranking last in expected goals against, with Swayman keeping playoff hopes alive.

title: Celtics legend Paul Pierce continues to make a case for Jaylen Brown as NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paul Pierce advocates for Jaylen Brown as a strong contender for NBA MVP due to his outstanding performance.
Jaylen Brown averages 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists this season, all career-highs for the Celtics forward.
Boston Celtics rank second in the Eastern Conference with a 47-23 record, aided by Brown's leadership and scoring prowess.

title: Plymouth police officer on paid leave amid criminal investigation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Plymouth Officer Samantha Pelrine is on paid leave amid a State Police criminal investigation into her off-duty conduct.
The investigation into Officer Pelrine is being conducted with no additional details shared due to its ongoing nature.
Plymouth Police Department reaffirms commitment to accountability, conducting an internal investigation alongside State Police's inquiry.

title: MLB writer predicts Roman Anthony will win AL MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Athletic's Eno Sarris predicts Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony will win the AL MVP for the 2026 MLB season.
Anthony had a strong rookie year with a .292 average, eight home runs, and 32 RBI despite an oblique injury.
His performance in the 2026 World Baseball Classic further proved his potential, batting .280 with two home runs for Team USA.

title: Pedestrian killed in Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was killed in a hit-and-run on Friday morning near Southampton Street and Bradston Street, Roxbury.
The victim was pronounced dead at a local hospital, Boston police confirmed.
The driver fled the scene; the vehicle was found later. No arrests have been made, and the investigation continues.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about chances for snow through Monday
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: The National Weather Service predicts a coating to 2 inches of wet snow, especially near I-90, by Monday.
Rain will begin this afternoon, turning to wet snow and sleet across Northern Massachusetts to Rhode Island by Monday morning.
Meteorologists forecast scattered snow showers Monday night, with accumulations reaching up to 2 inches in some areas.

title: Mass. police officer arrested for OUI, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Norwood police officer Shawn Willman was arrested and charged with operating a vehicle under the influence of alcohol.
Willman was off duty during the arrest and has been placed on administrative leave pending the case outcome.
Norwood Police Department is conducting an internal affairs investigation related to Willman's OUI charge.

title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, from Marston Mills, faces aggravated battery charges after stabbing an acquaintance at a Miami Beach party.
Brian Gordon suffered a stab wound to his abdomen, while Dickerson had a severe arm injury; both were hospitalized.
Witnesses claimed a dispute over women led to the altercation; a blood-covered knife was recovered at the scene.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution fell 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, marking their third loss in four games this season.
Revolution's Ilay Feingold scored first, but St. Louis City's Chris Durkin and Marcel Hartel secured the win.
Forward Leo Campana exited the match due to injury, with the severity to be determined after an MRI.
